name: Leni Riefenstahl

acts.description: Directed Triumph of the Will (1935), the definitive Nazi propaganda film glorifying Hitler
Used Romani concentration camp prisoners as unpaid extras in her film Tiefland
Close personal relationship with Hitler and Goebbels; lifelong denial of Nazi complicity

behavior_summary: Made the most effective propaganda film in history for the Third Reich. Used concentration camp prisoners as film extras. Denied culpability until she died at 101.

art.summary: Technically revolutionary filmmaker whose innovations in documentary and sports cinematography are still taught in film schools — alongside the context of what she used those skills to do.

accountability_events: 1948 denazification court classified her as a 'fellow traveller'
2003 died at age 101, still denying full complicity

verdict.blurb: She made Hitler look like a god and called it art. She used people from the camps as film extras and then claimed she didn't know. She lived 101 years and died unrepentant. The technique was genius. The purpose was genocide.
